prompt: Write a Python script that: (1) queries IntAct and BioGRID REST APIs for published Francisella tularensis–human protein interactions (any experimental method), (2) collects manually curated interactions from the PATRIC virulence factor database for F. tularensis Schu S4, (3) constructs a bipartite network of F. tularensis effector proteins and their human innate-immunity targets (TLR, NF-κB pathway, phagosome escape), (4) identifies host proteins that are targeted by multiple effectors (multi-targeted hubs), (5) annotates edges with experimental evidence type and PubMed ID, (6) outputs: interaction CSV, network GraphML, and a targeted pathway enrichment figure using matplotlib. Note: this analysis uses only publicly deposited experimental data to characterise host-pathogen interactions for therapeutic target identification.
source_citation: Derived from IntAct, BioGRID (2024_10) and PATRIC; target: F. tularensis effector-host network; organism: Francisella tularensis / Homo sapiens; catalogue version v1.0

_provenance.target_name: Francisella tularensis effector–host immune interaction network
_provenance.target_class: select agent bacterium–innate immunity protein interaction network
